serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Pork Ears Salad is approximately 150 g, equivalent to one small plate as commonly served in Singapore hawker centres or Burmese-style eateries.

nutrient_source.title: Pork Ears Salad (ဝက်နားရွက်ထောင်း) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for Pork Ears Salad (cr_id G00010618) with confidence 0.95. All per_100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this entry. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central pork ear data (USDA SR Legacy #10218) as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Pork Ears Salad. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous mixed pork and vegetable salad with fish sauce dressing. The dish has very low carbohydrate content (5.9 g/100g) dominated by simple sugars from dressing ingredients; the fat-dominant macronutrient profile (44.54 g fat/100g) substantially blunts postprandial glucose response. GL per 150 g serving = (45 × 5.9 × 1.5) / 100 ≈ 4.0 g, classified as low GL. Analogous estimate anchored to University of Sydney GI Database mixed salad with protein and fat entries.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 810 mg/100 g — well above the 600 mg/100 g threshold; contributed by fish sauce, dried shrimp, and brined pork ears
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Pork Ears Salad due to its very high sodium content of 810 mg/100 g; if consumed, portion should not exceed 50 g and fish sauce and dried shrimp quantities should be substantially reduced in preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Extremely high fat density (44.54 g/100 g) and caloric density (430 kcal/100 g) with zero dietary fibre impair insulin sensitivity and promote weight gain
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular consumption of Pork Ears Salad due to its very high fat content and caloric density combined with zero dietary fibre, which is metabolically unfavourable for glycaemic control and insulin sensitivity; if consumed, portion should be limited to no more than 50–75 g and paired with high-fibre vegetables.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (44.54 g/100 g) with estimated saturated fat ~12 g/100 g, high sodium (810 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (26 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Pork Ears Salad due to its high saturated fat, sodium, and caloric density, all of which adversely affect lipid profiles, blood pressure, and endothelial function; this dish is not recommended as a regular dietary component for those managing CVD risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory red meat (pork), high saturated fat (~12 g/100 g), high sodium (810 mg/100 g), and nightshade ingredient (chilli) collectively trigger multiple RA hard gates
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Pork Ears Salad as it contains pro-inflammatory red meat rich in arachidonic acid, estimated saturated fat far exceeding the 5 g/100 g threshold, high sodium that worsens tissue swelling, and chilli as a nightshade ingredient that may exacerbate joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pork Ears Salad (ဝက်နားရွက်ထောင်း, Wak Nar Ywe Htaung) is a Burmese-influenced cold salad dish found in Singapore's multicultural hawker food scene. It is prepared by boiling pork ears until tender, slicing them thinly to expose the characteristic gelatinous cartilage and skin layers, then tossing the slices in a pungent dressing of fish sauce, dried shrimp, lime juice, chilli, shallots, garlic, and sesame oil. The dish has a distinctive chewy-gelatinous texture from the collagen-rich cartilage of the pork ear, contrasted with the sharp, salty, and spicy flavour profile of the dressing. Nutritionally, the dish is extremely high in fat (44.54 g/100 g) and calories (430 kcal/100 g), with very low protein (1.32 g/100 g) relative to its caloric density, negligible dietary fibre (0 g/100 g), and very high sodium (810 mg/100 g). The high fat content is primarily attributable to the sesame oil and the natural fat content of pork ear skin. Clinically, this dish is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension (sodium 810 mg/100 g), Type 2 Diabetes (high fat, zero fibre, high caloric density), cardiovascular disease (high saturated fat and sodium), or rheumatoid arthritis (pro-inflammatory red meat, nightshade chilli, high sodium). It is typically served as a starter or side dish at lunch or dinner in Singapore hawker centres and Burmese-style restaurants. The dish is not halal due to pork content.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo Singapore database entry G00010618 with provenance marked as canonical and confidence 0.95. All core macros and available micronutrients are taken from this approved primary source. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA SR Legacy pork ear data as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown; this reduces confidence for that specific field only, not the overall record.
notes: dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB Foodo (confirmed zero per HPB source G00010618); this is plausible for a dish dominated by pork ear skin, sesame oil, and fish sauce dressing with minimal vegetable fibre. Saturated_fat_g estimated at 12.0 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #10218 (Pork, fresh, variety meats and by-products, ears, frozen, raw) which reports approximately 4.0 g saturated fat per 100 g raw ear; the high total fat of 44.54 g/100 g in the HPB entry (likely reflecting added sesame oil in dressing) implies a proportionally higher saturated fat contribution from the combined dish; 12 g/100 g is a conservative estimate. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown, vitamin profile, and moisture data for this composite Burmese-style pork ear salad preparation are not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. GI estimated at 45 (low) using analogous mixed salad with protein and fat from University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this dish. The dish contains pork and is not halal. Chilli is a nightshade ingredient relevant to rheumatoid arthritis monitoring.
